mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 216.71 (CPIAUCSL) signals central bank inaction on price pressures, eroding real equity returns through higher discount rates and negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 216.71.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in GDP (GDPC1) and rising unemployment (UNRATE) indicate recessionary pressures that reduce corporate earnings expectations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 falls > 1% YoY and UNRATE rises > 0.5pp
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where both GDPC1 declines and UNRATE rises.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in industrial production (INDPRO) and rising CPI (CPIAUCSL) signal stagflationary pressures that compress profit margins and drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where INDPRO declines and CPI rises.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) indicates robust labor market conditions that boost consumer spending and corporate revenues, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 60% of instances with PAYEMS growth above 100k.

mechanism_narrative: Below-trend housing starts (HOUST) signal weak residential demand, reducing construction activity and related sector earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 60% of instances where HOUST falls below 791.0.

mechanism_narrative: High consumer spending (RSAFS) combined with low interest rates (FEDFUNDS) creates a supportive environment for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 50% of instances where RSAFS exceeds 355655 and FEDFUNDS is below 0.39.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in GDP (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) indicate broad-based demand destruction, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days during economic contractions.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 falls > 1% YoY and HOUST declines > 5%
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where both GDPC1 and HOUST decline.
